channel.title: Comments for page "Do You Remember Funerals?"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Do You Remember Funerals?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I'm glad you enjoyed! :D

I am lost when it comes to the image interpretations in this tale. Is the the red hoe supposed to represent something he's been looking for, or the teddy bear?

So I would suggest reading the rest of the tales in the series. This should hopefully make it more clear what these images mean :). You can find the next tale here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for the cold post, but I am lost when it comes to the image interpretations in this tale. Is the the red hoe supposed to represent something he's been looking for, or the teddy bear? I am obviously missing something big, I could use a hint if anyone can give one.

Other than that the tale was great, the end with Emily begging for death was chilling. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Well… um, Emily we really need to go. Our flight's in an hour, I don't know if we're going to make it," Eric said as me moved toward the door.

"M E" IS DEATH, CONFIRMED. (I don't know what that line is supposed to be, but i hope someone knows)

item.title: Re: Grammatical Error
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the catch! Should be fixed now!

item.title: Grammatical Error
item.content:encoded:
"Joyce went to the top section of the refrigerator the they had bought…"

Just letting you know.

item.title: Re: Actually Following Through On A Series! Woot!
item.content:encoded:
Hurray for continuation! May the wheel grind on, and on.

+ A typo-ish thing:
- A typo-ish thing:

She was curious what could be so important that they'd call Emily during her in of all people.

Haven’t decided on a rating yet; I’ll get there. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still want a tale of O5-11 Roger Sheldon in this canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
End Of Death is the only hub that leaves me wanting more. And in a good way , the characters are flawed in their own beautiful ways. If immortality was forced upon the world, I think this is the way it would go down.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you enjoyed!

They combine good surreal imagery with enough coherence to get the gist of what Tony is trying to say.

This is actually one of the hardest challenges of writing those imaging reports. I've given myself a strict set of rules that like, I can never be explicit but everything has to have some purpose or reason. But sometimes the things you want to convey are really difficult to abstract out, so I'm glad they still felt coherent!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tbh, I'm not a fan of EOD, but despite my minor bias, i can bring myself to like this tale.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keep shining on, end of death is one of the best series on the site. Can't wait to keep reading it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoy the image logs in this tale. They combine good surreal imagery with enough coherence to get the gist of what Tony is trying to say. On top of that, the final scene with Emily is absolutely heart-wrenching.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, the thing is that the Foundation as an organization never knew about 3448. All of Project Dammerung was kept a secret, so when Emily and everyone was told to jump ship, they didn't do the most thorough clean up. Emily destroyed a lot of the evidence, but she didn't destroy the machine. And it's not like they could've moved it either, it's a massive machine. So the best course of action, since Emily didn't want to destroy it, was to just leave it there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the End of Death series, and this is a good read, but there is one major thing I can't wrap my head around. Why did the Foundation just leave SCP-3448 in an abandoned site? That doesn't seem like the sort of thing they'd do…like…at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*new pages*
“Do you remember funerals?
Remembers qk
Upvote
Edit: I’m it’s omega k but I mall it qk

item.title: Actually Following Through On A Series! Woot!
item.content:encoded:
So, as you can see this season will not be the same onslaught of content that the first season was, but I'll try not to let the time between segments get too long.

Thanks to Othellothecat, UraniumEmpire, Croquembouche, stormbreath, and Conwell for critique!

If you want to check out more stuff by me you can find it here and if you want to read other End Of Death material you can go to the End Of Death Hub!

Image attribution: By LearningLark [CC BY 2.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0)], via Wikimedia Commons

Thanks for reading!
